ROYAL PALM BEACH HEALTH AND REHABILITATION CENTER

CCN: 105494 · ROYAL PALM BEACH, FL 33411 · Palm Beach County

Overview

Address
600 BUSINESS PARK WAY, ROYAL PALM BEACH, FL 33411
Phone
5617983700
Certified beds
120
Avg daily residents
104 (87% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1985-02-07
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.34 3.68 3.50 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.67 0.64 0.70 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.60 0.82 0.63 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.07 2.22 2.17 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.27 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.15
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.57
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.30
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
35.4%
annual
RN turnover
33.3%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.30
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-22 9 9 0 40 1 40
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-08 7 7 0 32 1 32
Total weighted health score
38.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (28)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
27 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2025-05-22 2025-06-20
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0917 Make sure each resident has 1) at least one window to the outside in a room; 2) a room at or above ground level; 3) adequate bedding; 4) furniture that meets the resident's needs; or 5) adequate closet space. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-01
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0885 Report COVID19 data to residents and families. C 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2022-09-23 2022-10-23
